title: El-Qess Restaurant AI Assistant
subtitle: Langflow-Powered Moroccan Restaurant AI with RAG and MCP Integrations
description: An AI-powered virtual assistant for an authentic Moroccan restaurant built with Langflow. Handles FAQ answering via RAG, table reservations through Google Calendar MCP, and sends automated email confirmations via Gmail MCP, all through a conversational interface.
longDescription:
El-Qess Restaurant AI Assistant is a fully visual AI agent built on **Langflow** that handles the three core operations of a Moroccan restaurant: answering customer questions, managing reservations, and sending confirmation emails.

For FAQ answering, the agent uses a **RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)** pipeline. Restaurant documents are loaded, chunked with a Recursive Text Splitter, converted into vectors using **OpenAI Embeddings**, and stored in **Chroma DB**. When a customer asks a question, the agent performs a semantic search and generates a grounded, accurate answer.

For reservations, the agent collects all required guest details through conversation (name, date, time, number of guests, email, and phone), then uses **Google Calendar MCP** via Composio to create the calendar event automatically. Once the booking is confirmed, **Gmail MCP** sends a formatted confirmation email directly to the guest.

The entire workflow is built visually in Langflow without custom code, demonstrating how drag-and-drop AI orchestration can produce a production-ready hospitality assistant. The agent uses MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers via Composio for both Google Calendar and Gmail integrations, handling OAuth 2.0 authentication transparently.

title: Twitter MCP Server
subtitle: Model Context Protocol Server for Twitter/X Automation
description: A production-ready Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that enables AI agents and automation workflows to post tweets, publish threads, and search Twitter/X. Built with FastMCP and Python, it exposes a clean tool interface compatible with any MCP client including Claude, n8n, and LangGraph agents.
longDescription:
Twitter MCP Server is a production-ready **MCP (Model Context Protocol)** server that bridges AI agents with Twitter/X, exposing a clean tool interface compatible with any MCP-compliant platform including Claude, n8n, and LangGraph agents.

The server provides four tools:

- **post_tweet** for single tweets with optional reply threading
- **post_thread** for publishing connected multi-tweet threads in one call
- **search_tweets** for querying recent tweets with full Twitter operator support
- **get_user_profile** for retrieving authenticated user metadata

It supports dual transport endpoints: Streamable HTTP (`/mcp`) and Server-Sent Events (`/sse`), with remote access via **ngrok** tunneling for cloud-based AI platforms.

The demo showcases **TrendToTweet**, an AI content agent built on top of this server. Given a user topic, the agent searches Google and YouTube for trending content from the last 7 days, extracts insights from the best sources, generates a structured blog post hosted at a temporary URL, crafts a curiosity-driven tweet with the blog link, and publishes it live to Twitter, all autonomously. TrendToTweet is simply one example of what you can build with this MCP server.

title: Project Architect Agent
subtitle: LangGraph-Powered Automated Project Documentation Workflow
description: Autonomous AI agent that transforms project ideas into structured Notion workspaces through automated research and documentation generation using LangChain and LangGraph.
longDescription:
The Project Architect Agent is an autonomous AI research and documentation assistant that takes a project idea and produces a fully structured **Notion workspace**, automatically, end to end.

The system first extracts key metadata from the idea (title, objectives, deliverables), then generates 4-7 actionable implementation steps. For each step, the agent enters a research loop: it reflects on what information is needed, searches the web via **Google Custom Search** or **DuckDuckGo**, reads relevant URLs, synthesizes findings using an LLM, and writes structured documentation directly into Notion.

The architecture follows **LangGraph** state machine patterns with modular nodes: . A typed state schema ensures clean data flow between nodes, and services for research, synthesis, and Notion integration are fully separated.

The system supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoint including OAI, Ollama, vLLM, and LM Studio. A **Gradio** interface provides real-time progress updates throughout the generation process.

title: AI Invoice Processing Agent
subtitle: LangGraph-Powered Automated Invoice Approval Workflow
description: AI agent that extracts, validates, and auto-approves or rejects PDF invoices using LangChain and LangGraph. Enforces business rules, validates calculations, and sends automated email notifications with a Gradio web interface.
longDescription:
The AI Invoice Processing Agent is a production-ready agentic workflow that fully automates the invoice approval lifecycle using **LangGraph** state machine patterns and **GPT-4** for structured data extraction.

The system ingests PDF invoices and extracts all structured fields (vendor, line items, totals, and tax), then validates calculations mathematically and enforces custom business policies:

- Field requirements and amount limits
- Duplicate detection
- Automated approval or rejection with clear explanations
- Email notifications via Gmail for every decision

The architecture uses modular LangGraph nodes for each pipeline stage: PDF ingestion, LLM extraction, validation, decision, and notification. A typed state schema with **Pydantic** enforces LLM output contracts throughout. The project supports any OpenAI-compatible endpoint including Azure, Gemini, Claude, and local Ollama models, and is fully configurable via `.env`. A **Gradio** interface provides drag-and-drop invoice upload with real-time processing feedback.

title: AL-MUQARRIR
subtitle: Automatic Transcription & Meeting Minutes Generation
description: AI-powered web platform for real-time meeting transcription, speaker diarization, and automated generation of meeting minutes with semantic analysis. Supports Arabic, French, English, and Darija with multilingual and RTL capabilities.
longDescription:
AL-MUQARRIR is a comprehensive AI platform that transforms meeting recordings into structured, professional documentation, supporting Arabic, French, English, and Darija with full RTL capabilities.

Speaker identification uses **PyAnnote.audio** in real time, while **OpenAI Whisper** delivers high-accuracy multilingual transcription. **Google Gemini API** then performs intelligent semantic analysis to automatically generate meeting minutes with theme extraction, sentiment analysis, and identification of problems and opportunities.

The platform exports seamlessly to PDF and DOCX formats and supports the full Arabic RTL text formatting standard. Built with an **Angular** frontend and a **Flask** backend with **JWT** authentication and **SQLite** storage, AL-MUQARRIR covers the entire meeting documentation workflow from raw recording to formatted, distributable minutes.

title: Cura
subtitle: AI-Powered Medical Management System
description: Comprehensive Flask-based web application for managing patient medications, pathologies, and appointments with AI-powered drug-drug interaction (DDI) detection using machine learning and Google Gemini AI. Features medication tracking, mentor-patient relationships, pharmacy management, and intelligent medication safety checks.
longDescription:
Cura is a healthcare management platform that combines medication tracking with AI-powered drug interaction detection, built to enhance patient safety and simplify medical management.

Drug interaction analysis uses a two-tier approach: a local **PyTorch MLP** neural network with molecular fingerprints from **RDKit** for probability-based detection, and **Google Gemini AI** for generating patient-friendly safety alerts and contextual medical advice. Molecular data is fetched from **PubChem** for real-time DDI analysis.

Patients can manage medications with dosage tracking, set automated reminders, monitor pathologies, and schedule appointments. A mentorship system connects patients with mentors for ongoing support, while advanced autocomplete with fuzzy matching helps users find medications quickly.

The backend is built with **Flask** and **SQLAlchemy**, providing role-based access control, secure password hashing with Bcrypt, and comprehensive medication intake logging. This project demonstrates the application of deep learning and generative AI to real-world healthcare challenges.

title: Real-Time Network Intrusion Detection System
subtitle: ML-Powered Network Security Monitoring
description: Real-time network intrusion detection system that captures and analyzes network packets using machine learning. Built with Apache Kafka for message streaming, PyShark for packet capture, and Scikit-learn for traffic classification. The system provides continuous network monitoring with automated threat detection and comprehensive logging.
longDescription:
This real-time network intrusion detection system captures live network packets, analyzes them with machine learning models, and logs results for security auditing, built to scale for enterprise network monitoring.

**Apache Kafka** handles reliable, high-throughput message delivery across the pipeline. **PyShark** captures packets from network interfaces and extracts features such as protocol types, packet sizes, and timing information. The preprocessed data is fed into a **Scikit-learn** classifier that predicts traffic types and flags potential threats including DoS attacks, port scans, and unauthorized access attempts.

Predictions are automatically saved to Excel files for historical analysis and audit trails. The system supports continuous monitoring with role-based access to archived logs, making it suitable for security operations teams that need both real-time detection and long-term forensic capabilities.

title: LipReader
subtitle: AI-Powered Lip Reading Application
description: Deep learning application that converts lip movements into text using advanced computer vision and neural networks. Built with TensorFlow for model implementation and Streamlit for an interactive user interface. The system employs 3D convolutional neural networks combined with LSTM layers to accurately predict text from video-based lip movements.
longDescription:
LipReader is a deep learning application that translates silent lip movements from video into readable text, built as a proof-of-concept for assistive technology and accessibility use cases.

The model architecture combines **3D Convolutional Neural Networks (3D-CNN)** for spatial feature extraction from video frames with **LSTM** layers for temporal sequence modeling. This hybrid approach captures both the visual shape of lips and the temporal dynamics of speech patterns, enabling accurate text predictions from video input.

Built on **TensorFlow**, the model processes video frame by frame, extracting facial features and generating character predictions. The **Streamlit** interface lets users upload videos or select pre-loaded samples and see real-time lip reading results. The system includes video preprocessing utilities, character mapping functions, and alignment data processing to ensure high accuracy.

This project demonstrates expertise in computer vision, sequence-to-sequence modeling, and the potential of deep learning to help individuals with hearing impairments through silent communication systems.

title: NER - Named Entity Recognition
subtitle: Multi-Model Entity Detection System
description: Advanced Named Entity Recognition application that identifies and classifies entities in text using multiple deep learning and NLP models. Built with Streamlit for an interactive interface, the system employs Keras-based neural networks, spaCy, and Stanza to extract entities such as persons, organizations, locations, and miscellaneous categories from unstructured text.
longDescription:
This **Named Entity Recognition** system uses multiple state-of-the-art NLP models to identify and classify entities in unstructured text with high accuracy.

Four models run simultaneously and present comparative results through a **Streamlit** interface:

- **Keras (model 1)** specializes in geographical entities, organizations, and persons using custom embedding layers
- **Keras (model 2)** focuses on miscellaneous entities and locations with enhanced classification
- **spaCy** provides fast and accurate entity recognition using pre-trained language models
- **Stanza** (Stanford NLP) adds robust multilingual capabilities

The application includes text preprocessing, tokenization, and entity boundary detection. Displaying results from all four models side by side lets users validate entity extraction across frameworks, giving higher confidence for document analysis, information extraction, content categorization, and automated text annotation tasks.

title: Anxiety Level Prediction
subtitle: ML-Based Mental Health Assessment Platform
description: Comprehensive platform for analyzing and predicting anxiety levels based on psychological traits, demographic factors, and behavioral information. Built with Python and Scikit-learn, the system employs multiple machine learning models to classify anxiety levels using Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD) questionnaire responses. Features a Flask backend and Bootstrap frontend for an interactive user experience.
longDescription:
This mental health assessment platform uses **machine learning** to predict anxiety levels from psychological, demographic, and behavioral data collected through a **Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD)** questionnaire.

The data science pipeline covers missing value handling, categorical feature encoding with **LabelEncoder**, and feature engineering to extract meaningful patterns. Multiple algorithms are evaluated, including **Random Forest**, **SVM**, and **Gradient Boosting**, through cross-validation to find the best performer. Hyperparameter tuning with **GridSearchCV** further optimizes the final SVM model.

Data visualizations built with **Matplotlib** and **Seaborn** illustrate age distributions and categorical relationships with anxiety levels. The **Flask** backend exposes RESTful prediction endpoints, while a **Bootstrap** frontend delivers an intuitive questionnaire experience with real-time predictions and personalized mental health recommendations.

The **ModelPipeline** class encapsulates all preprocessing and prediction logic for clean deployment. This project demonstrates how data science can support accessible mental health screening and wellness applications.

title: Fake News Detection
subtitle: ML-Powered News Authenticity Classifier
description: Machine learning system that classifies news articles as fake or real using natural language processing and logistic regression. Built with Python and Scikit-learn, the system employs TF-IDF vectorization and text preprocessing techniques including stemming and stopword removal to analyze news content from a Kaggle competition dataset with thousands of labeled articles.
longDescription:
This fake news detection system uses **machine learning** and **natural language processing** to classify news articles as reliable or unreliable, addressing the critical challenge of misinformation.

The pipeline processes a large-scale Kaggle dataset containing articles with author, title, and body text. All three fields are merged into a unified content column before preprocessing. Text is cleaned using **Porter Stemming** to reduce words to root forms, stopword removal to eliminate uninformative words, and filtering of non-alphabetic characters.

**TF-IDF vectorization** then converts the preprocessed text into numerical features that capture word importance across the corpus. A **logistic regression** model is trained on these features, chosen for its interpretability and strong performance on binary classification tasks, and achieves high accuracy on both training and test sets.

The system includes a predictive function that accepts any new article and returns a real-time authenticity prediction, making it ready to integrate into news platforms, fact-checking tools, or social media monitoring workflows.

title: GESTAG
subtitle: Intern and Internship Management System
description: Comprehensive web application for managing trainees, internships, and registrations developed with Java EE and Oracle database. The system streamlines the entire internship lifecycle from stage selection to trainee registration, featuring dynamic sorting, waitlist management, and automated status tracking. Built with a modern frontend using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, and designed in Figma.
longDescription:
GESTAG is a robust enterprise-level web application built to manage the complete lifecycle of trainees, internships, and registration processes for educational institutions. It is built on **Java EE** with an **Oracle** relational database backend, implementing sophisticated business logic through stored procedures and functions in **PL/SQL**.

The application features a multi-window interface starting with secure authentication, followed by an intelligent stage selection screen displaying available internships with dynamic sorting by type or start date. Users can view detailed stage information including registered trainees and available slots.

The registration workflow handles automatic trainee creation with full data validation and duplicate checking. **Capacity management** is built-in: the system automatically assigns trainees to a waiting list when an internship is full, or confirms registration when slots are available, then updates all counts and maintains data integrity across related tables.

The six application windows cover: login, stage selection, stage details, registration, new trainee creation, and confirmation. The frontend was designed in **Figma** and implemented with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, while the backend leverages Java EE for robust server-side processing.
